name: Legal Prose
description: Styled legal documents with auto-generated TOC, smooth scroll navigation, collapsible sections, and print-friendly layout.

cookies.description: Cookie policy with collapsible category sections

visual_brief: Long-form legal document layout with a sticky table of contents sidebar on the left and the prose content area on the right. The TOC auto-generates from heading hierarchy with indented sub-sections, highlighting the current section on scroll. The prose area uses comfortable reading typography — constrained line length, generous line height, numbered section headers, and proper paragraph spacing. A header shows the document title and 'Last updated' date. Footer contains contact information and version history link. The document is print-friendly with clean page breaks.

layout_hints.measure: Keep prose readable with a constrained content column and do not let legal copy stretch edge-to-edge across the viewport.
layout_hints.toc_behavior: The table of contents is supportive chrome, not the hero. It should collapse cleanly on smaller screens without obscuring the legal text.
layout_hints.print_rhythm: Headings, paragraphs, and section breaks should preserve a sober, stable rhythm in both screen and print contexts.

responsive.mobile: TOC collapses into a dropdown menu at the top. Prose takes full width with comfortable reading margins. Sections stack with clear heading hierarchy.
responsive.tablet: TOC as a collapsible left drawer. Prose area takes remaining width with comfortable reading column.
responsive.desktop: Full two-column layout — sticky TOC sidebar on the left (220px), prose content on the right with max-width for readability.

motion.micro: TOC highlight changes and smooth-scroll anchors should feel restrained and utility-first.
motion.transitions: Collapsible category sections and TOC drawers may animate over 180-220ms, but legal reading surfaces should avoid decorative motion.

keyboard: Tab moves through the table of contents and in-document links in source order.
Keyboard users should be able to reach section headings and jump links without losing context.
announcements: Document title, effective date, and section headings should be available through semantic heading structure.
accessibility.focus_management: When TOC links are activated, move focus to the destination heading or ensure the destination heading is programmatically associated with the jump target.
